title: Kyrstenia guadalupensis
taxonomy: Kyrstenia guadalupensis is classified as a species within the family Annonaceae, which comprises flowering plants commonly known as custard apples. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its relationship to other members of the Annonaceae family. The Annonaceae family is part of the larger order Magnoliales, which includes flowering plants that are often characterized by their distinctive flowers and fruit. The order Magnoliales is part of the clade Magnoliidae, which comprises flowering plants that are often associated with ancient lineages. Kyrstenia guadalupensis is a tropical plant species native to the island of Guadalupe, which is located in the Caribbean.
uses: Kyrstenia guadalupensis is a plant species that is native to Mexico and Central America. Unfortunately, information on the medicinal uses of Kyrtenia guadalupensis is limited.
